event: diagnosed with acetylcholine receptor antibody positive oculo-bulbar myasthenia gravis
aspiration to thin liquids on modified barium swallow
started on pyridostigmine 60 mg 4 times daily
started on regular plasma exchange, 3 exchanges every 4 weeks
CT chest showed 6.7 cm lobulated soft tissue mass in anterior mediastinum
referred to cardiothoracic surgery for possible thymectomy
presented to emergency department with fever
cough with minimal clear sputum production
no history of exposure to sick
no history of similar symptoms in friends or family
chest x-ray showed patchy infiltrates in left lower lobe
return to emergency department if symptoms worsened
